scenarios.opening_query: Someone slashed my tires twice this month and I have a good idea who it is.
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's my wife's ex-husband — he did the same to her car before ours; she and I both need protection, and he's her former spouse.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's my wife's ex-husband — he did her car first. We both need protection, and he's her former spouse.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: the relationship between the protected persons and the actor
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's a man from my gym who's fixated on my parking spot feud with him — no relationship whatsoever.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: A guy from my gym fixated on our parking feud — no relationship at all.
scenarios.vetting_note: Derivative-FAPA nuance: her relationship qualifies her petition; his conduct against the household is the practical case.

scenarios.opening_query: I need a protection order against my daughter's boyfriend before he does something worse to her.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Family Law > Restraining Orders || Family Law > Juvenile/DHS Issues
scenarios.hidden_fact: She's 15 and he's 19 — the school reported it and DHS and police are now involved; the protective piece runs through their case, and she can't 'consent' to any of it.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: She's 15, he's 19 — the school reported it, and DHS and police are involved now. The protection runs through their case.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether minor status and agency involvement control the process
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: She's 22 and living at home — she wants the order herself against her ex who lived here with us for a year.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: She's 22 and home with us — she wants the order herself against the ex who lived here a year.
scenarios.vetting_note: Minor-victim facts reroute to the child-protection system.

scenarios.opening_query: A man I met online turned frightening when I stopped responding, and now he references my workplace.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Stalking Orders || General Litigation > Online Harrassment/Doxing/Bullying
scenarios.hidden_fact: He's three states away and everything is digital — posts, emails, fake accounts; the platform-takedown and unmasking work matters more than a local order he'd never see.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: He's three states away — all digital: posts, emails, fake accounts. Takedowns and unmasking matter more than a local order he'd never see.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the actor is remote making platform remedies primary
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: He's local — I've seen his car outside my office twice, and the stalking order is what stands between us.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: He's local — his car's been outside my office twice. The order is what matters.

scenarios.opening_query: My ex keeps showing up wherever I am with the baby and I'm done pretending it's coincidence.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Family Law > Restraining Orders || Family Law > Child Custody/Visitation
scenarios.hidden_fact: There's no custody order at all yet — he's the legal father and technically has equal rights to her until a court says otherwise; the custody case has to come first, with protective terms inside it.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: There's no custody order — he's the legal father with equal rights until a court says otherwise. The custody case comes first, with protective terms in it.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the absence of any custody order changes the remedy
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: We have a full custody order he's violating — and last week he grabbed my arm at the exchange; FAPA protection is the ask.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: We have a custody order he's violating — and he grabbed my arm last week. FAPA protection is the ask.
scenarios.vetting_note: No-order-yet posture is the underrated fact in new-parent separations.

scenarios.opening_query: The man in the unit above mine watches me through the window across the courtyard and has started leaving 'gifts.'
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Stalking Orders || Real Property > Tenant (Residential)
scenarios.hidden_fact: Management knows — three women reported him before me, and the landlord renewed his lease anyway; alongside my order, I want the landlord held to their safety obligations or my lease broken.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Management knows — three women reported him first, and they renewed his lease anyway. I want the landlord held to their obligations or my lease broken.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the landlord's knowing inaction creates a housing claim
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Management is cooperating fully — cameras, transfer offer — the order against him is all that's left.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Management's cooperating — cameras and a transfer offer. The order is all that's left.
scenarios.vetting_note: Landlord-knowledge fact adds the habitability/safety route.

scenarios.opening_query: My mother's ex keeps driving past her house since she ended it, and she's afraid to be alone.
scenarios.hidden_fact: They dated eight weeks and never lived together — the advocate says that doesn't reach the family-abuse statute here, so it's the stalking order route for her.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: They dated eight weeks and never lived together — the advocate says that misses the family-abuse statute, so it's the stalking order.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the brief relationship meets FAPA thresholds
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: They lived together two years — squarely inside the family-abuse statute.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: They lived together two years — squarely inside the statute.
scenarios.vetting_note: Relationship-duration threshold; advocate framing keeps the label defensible.
